learning_outcomes.description: Identify key structures and landmarks associated with the abdominal and pelvic cavities.
Identify and describe the structure and function of the gastrointestinal tract of domestic animals, highlighting differences between domestic animal species.

Develop an understanding of the ethical responsibilities and OH&S requirements associated with animal dissection and demonstrate proficiency in key dissection and anatomical identification skills that underpin animal surgery.

Demonstrate a strong understanding of the nutritional and metabolic requirements of domestic animals, and apply anatomical, physiological, nutritional and biochemical knowledge of the gastrointestinal tract to address and solve clinical problems in these species.

assessments.due_date:
Teeth/Skulls 7/08/2026 - 13/08/2026

Dog/cat dissection (Group B) 21/08/2026 - 27/08/2026

Ruminants 28/08/2026 - 3/09/2026

Pig 4/09/2026 - 10/09/2026

Horse teeth 18/09/2026 - 24/09/2026

Histology 7/10/2026 - 13/10/2026

All Blackboard quizzes are due at 5:00PM on final day listed as open. Please refer to VETS1003 Blackboard site for more information.
assessments.task_description:
The integrated assessment sequence will consist of six quizzes. The quizzes will last for fifteen (15) minutes each, will be open during the practical class and will remain open for the duration of ONE (1) week. Quizzes will be delivered through the Inspera platform or paper-based, depending on what best suits the question format. Please check the course Blackboard site regularly for details regarding this assessment item.

Although six quizzes will be conducted throughout the semester, the best FOUR (4) results attained by each student will be attributed to the final mark of this assessment item.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 7(27 Jul - 13 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Gastrointestinal anatomy

Structure and function of the gastrointestinal tract including: oral cavity and salivary glands, teeth, stomach and secretory glands, intestines and distal tract (rectum & anus), mesenteries, embryological development, liver and gall bladder, pancreas, histology of the GIT and comparative anatomy (pig, ruminants, horse).
learning_activities.topic_description: Gastrointestinal anatomy Structure and function of the gastrointestinal tract including: oral cavity and salivary glands, teeth, stomach and secretory glands, intestines and distal tract (rectum & anus), mesenteries, embryological development, liver and gall bladder, pancreas, histology of the GIT and comparative anatomy (pig, ruminants, horse).

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 3 To Week 12(10 Aug - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Gastrointestinal anatomy

Structure and function of the gastrointestinal tract including: oral cavity and salivary glands, stomach and secretory glands, intestines and lower tract, mesenteries, liver and gall bladder, pancreas, histology, comparative anatomy and evolution. Dissection of abdominal musculature and organs of abdomen and pelvis. Histology practical classes may employ VIRTUAL MICROSCOPY, BEST, and SLICE as learning tools.
learning_activities.topic_description: Gastrointestinal anatomy Structure and function of the gastrointestinal tract including: oral cavity and salivary glands, stomach and secretory glands, intestines and lower tract, mesenteries, liver and gall bladder, pancreas, histology, comparative anatomy and evolution. Dissection of abdominal musculature and organs of abdomen and pelvis. Histology practical classes may employ VIRTUAL MICROSCOPY, BEST, and SLICE as learning tools.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 7 To Week 9(07 Sep - 27 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Gastrointestinal physiology

The process of digestion and absorption in ruminants and monogastric animals, including control, motility, secretions, and digestive and absorptive processes.
learning_activities.topic_description: Gastrointestinal physiology The process of digestion and absorption in ruminants and monogastric animals, including control, motility, secretions, and digestive and absorptive processes.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 9 To Week 12(21 Sep - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Nutritional metabolism (biochemistry)

Mechanisms that animals use to control, regulate, and integrate metabolism to meet their varying needs, from times when nutrients are plentiful such that energy reserves can be replenished, through periods of fasting and scarcity when these reserves must be mobilised.
learning_activities.topic_description: Nutritional metabolism (biochemistry) Mechanisms that animals use to control, regulate, and integrate metabolism to meet their varying needs, from times when nutrients are plentiful such that energy reserves can be replenished, through periods of fasting and scarcity when these reserves must be mobilised.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 12 To Week 13(19 Oct - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Nutrition in domestic animals

Nutritional strategies and adaptations of different animals. Nutrient requirements, the provision of nutrients, regulation of nutrient supply and its utilisation. Feed composition and evaluation and why nutrition is relevant in performance and in disease.
learning_activities.topic_description: Nutrition in domestic animals Nutritional strategies and adaptations of different animals. Nutrient requirements, the provision of nutrients, regulation of nutrient supply and its utilisation. Feed composition and evaluation and why nutrition is relevant in performance and in disease.
